title: Empty states for menus, sections, pages, and editor content
description: Per `plano-de-interface.md`'s `# Componentes > Empty State` section ('Quando não existir conteúdo, mostrar uma ilustração simples. Título. Descrição. Botão: Adicionar conteúdo'), add proper empty states for the gaps the redesign surfaces: a menu with no sections yet (new, since TASK-83/84 introduced Menu), a section with no pages yet, a page with no content blocks yet, and (if TASK-91 lands search) a search-with-no-results state. Today these cases render bare/minimal (e.g. an empty sidebar group, or presumably a blank Tiptap editor).
acceptanceCriteria: A reusable `EmptyState` component exists: simple illustration/icon (lucide-react, consistent with the rest of the admin — no new illustration asset pipeline needed), title, short description, and a primary action button, per the plan's spec

A newly-created (or otherwise empty) menu with zero sections shows this empty state in the main content area when selected as active, with the action creating the first section (`sections.create` with that menu's id, per TASK-84/86)

A section with zero pages shows this empty state in the main content area when navigated to (coordinate with TASK-86's sidebar/section-navigation behavior for what a section with no pages navigates to), with the action creating the first page

A page/tab with zero content blocks shows this empty state inside the editor area instead of a bare blank editor, with the action inserting the first block (reusing TASK-88's block-insert entry point)
Empty states use TASK-82's typography tokens for title/description sizing, staying visually consistent with the rest of the redesign
No change to behavior when content DOES exist — this task only adds the zero-state branches

steps.details: Generic component: icon slot, title, description, action button (as a prop, so callers wire their own mutation).

When the active menu (TASK-85) resolves to zero sections via `sections.listByMenu`, render `EmptyState` with a 'Criar primeira seção' action in the main content area, instead of an empty sidebar with nothing to click into.

Wherever TASK-86's sidebar/navigation routes an empty section (or the main content area when a section has 0 pages), render `EmptyState` with a 'Criar primeira página' action calling the existing `pages.create` mutation.

Inside `ContentEditor`/`PageContentPage`, detect an empty document and render `EmptyState` with an 'Adicionar conteúdo' action that opens TASK-88's block picker (falling back to the existing top toolbar's insert action if TASK-88 hasn't landed).

Confirm each empty state disappears immediately after the first menu/section/page/block is created (no stale UI, no extra reload needed) — this exercises the same query invalidation already used throughout the app.

technicalNotes: Keep illustrations simple (a lucide icon in a soft circle, per common empty-state patterns) — the plan explicitly says 'ilustração simples', not a custom SVG library.

If TASK-88 (block toolbar) hasn't landed when this task is picked up, the page-empty-state action can fall back to the existing TASK-79 toolbar's insert button; don't block this task on TASK-88's completion, just note the dependency is soft for step 4.

The menu-empty-state case is new (added when Menu was confirmed as a real entity) — a fresh instance always has at least the TASK-83 default menu, but it starts with zero sections until the operator creates one, so this state is reachable on day one, not just a hypothetical.
